title: Hybrid Quantum Neighborhood Selection: NISQ-Compatible Combinatorial Optimization via Stochastic Frontier Decomposition
abstract: Large-scale combinatorial optimization is a challenge for near-term quantum computing because dense Quadratic Unconstrained Binary Optimization (QUBO) formulations yield interaction graphs that exceed the limits of NISQ processors. This work introduces Hybrid Quantum Neighborhood Selection (HQNS), a hybrid framework mitigating this via stochastic frontier decomposition. Instead of encoding all N variables into a monolithic circuit, HQNS selects a compact frontier of F << N active variables per stage, freezing the rest into reduced QUBO coefficients. A multi-stage crawling procedure rotates these frontiers, letting local quantum subproblems refine a global solution. We evaluate HQNS on the Maximum Diversity Subset Selection Problem (MDSSP) across six scales, N up to 1000. Circuit burden is reduced from the dense QAOA requirement of O(N^2) two-qubit terms per layer to O(F^2) per stage, with total complexity governed by the number of stages and classical overhead. Benchmarks show that HQNS achieves competitive solution quality relative to parallel simulated annealing (SA) while maintaining bounded circuit width and stable QPU time. In the N=1000 benchmark over ten executions, HQNS preserves 99.9908% of the mean diversity score of an 11-restart parallel SA baseline, while reducing wall-clock time by 65.03%, peak CPU usage by 55.97%, and peak memory by 35.21%. Ablation shows performance depends on frontier size, warm-starts, CVaR filtering, and stochastic rotation. These results demonstrate that structured frontier decomposition makes variational optimization executable for dense QUBO instances unsuitable for direct QAOA on present hardware.
summary: 该论文提出了一种名为“混合量子邻域选择”（HQNS）的混合框架，旨在解决近量子处理器（NISQ）无法直接处理大规模组合优化问题（如最大多样性子集选择问题）的困境。HQNS通过随机前沿分解，将整体优化问题分解为一系列小规模的量子子问题（前沿变量F << N），每次只优化一小部分变量，其余变量被冻结并转化为经典系数。通过多阶段爬虫过程，前沿不断旋转，使得局部量子子问题（如QAOA）逐步改进全局解。实验表明，在N=1000的规模下，HQNS在保持与并行模拟退火相当的解质量的同时，将电路宽度从O(N²)降低到O(F²)，并显著缩短了计算时间、降低了CPU和内存使用。该工作属于量子计算与经典优化的混合方法，主要面向NISQ时代的实用化量子优化。
chinese_digest:
## 中文导读
- 研究问题：如何让NISQ量子处理器处理大规模组合优化问题（如最大多样性子集选择）。
- 核心方法：提出混合量子邻域选择（HQNS），通过随机前沿分解将大问题分解为小规模量子子问题，每次只优化前沿变量，其余冻结为经典系数，通过多阶段迭代逐步逼近全局最优。
- 主要结果：在N=1000的基准测试中，HQNS保持了与11次重启并行模拟退火99.9908%的平均多样性分数，同时减少65.03%的墙钟时间、55.97%的CPU峰值和35.21%的内存峰值，电路复杂度从O(N²)降至O(F²)。
- 与量子精密测量/量子计算的关系：该论文属于量子计算优化算法领域，与量子精密测量、量子传感、Rydberg原子、中性原子量子计算、离子阱、Fisher信息等方向无直接关联。它不涉及任何量子测量协议、量子Fisher信息、自旋压缩或原子钟等主题。
- 是否值得精读：对于专注于量子精密测量或量子传感的研究者，不建议精读。对于从事量子优化算法或NISQ应用的研究者，可能有参考价值。
- 对我研究的可能启发：如果研究涉及量子计算在组合优化中的应用，该混合方法可能提供一种降低硬件需求的思路，但对本兴趣领域（量子精密测量、量子传感）的启发非常有限。
english_digest:
## English Guide
- Problem: How to enable NISQ quantum processors to handle large-scale combinatorial optimization problems (e.g., Maximum Diversity Subset Selection).
- Method: Proposes Hybrid Quantum Neighborhood Selection (HQNS), which uses stochastic frontier decomposition to break the problem into small quantum subproblems. Only a frontier of F << N variables is optimized per stage; the rest are frozen and converted into classical coefficients. A multi-stage crawling procedure rotates the frontier to refine a global solution.
- Main result: On N=1000 benchmarks, HQNS preserves 99.9908% of the mean diversity score of an 11-restart parallel simulated annealing baseline, while reducing wall-clock time by 65.03%, peak CPU usage by 55.97%, and peak memory by 35.21%. Circuit complexity is reduced from O(N²) to O(F²).
- Relevance: This paper focuses on quantum combinatorial optimization and has no direct connection to quantum precision measurement, quantum sensing, Rydberg atoms, neutral-atom quantum computing, trapped ions, Fisher information, spin squeezing, or atomic clocks. It does not address any metrology or sensing protocols.
- Reading priority: Low for researchers in quantum metrology, quantum sensing, or atomic physics. May be of interest to those working on NISQ quantum optimization algorithms.
- Possible inspiration: The hybrid decomposition approach could inspire similar strategies for reducing hardware requirements in quantum optimization, but offers little to no insight for quantum sensing or precision measurement.
